text: The catalytic, enantioselective radical hydroamination of enol esters with sulfonamides was achieved under the combined action of an Ir photocatalyst, a Brønsted acid, and a tetrapeptide thiol. The approach enabled the efficient preparation of 23 protected β-amino-alcohol derivatives with er values up to 97:3. High levels of stereocontrol (above 95 % re) were achieved by generating the stereocentred product via a selective hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) from the chiral thiol catalyst to the prochiral C-centred radical within the reaction cascade. By elucidating the structure-reactivity relationships derived from the variation of catalyst components versus olefin substrate, we obtained insight into design of catalyst system. The mechanistic experiments along with corresponding computational modelling established the role of various noncovalent interactions, including hydrogen bonding, π-π stacking as well as London dispersion, in high fidelity catalyst-olefin substrate recognition, which leads to the high level of enantioinduction achieved within the radical-based asymmetric catalysis approach.
